issues.description: Harden phase has no max_rounds fallback after the judge match. In the review path (line 1018), if the judge returns `continue` or is unavailable at max_rounds, the code checks `record.round >= record.max_rounds` and transitions to Failed. The harden path has no equivalent check — after the judge match falls through (continue or None), it calls `dispatch_revise` unconditionally, allowing the loop to exceed max_rounds indefinitely when the judge is disabled, errors out, or returns `continue` at the boundary. The review path handles this correctly; harden does not.
issues.suggestion: Add a max_rounds check between the judge match block and the `dispatch_revise` call in the harden evaluation, mirroring the review path: `if record.round >= record.max_rounds { record.state = LoopState::Failed; ... return Ok(LoopState::Failed); }`

issues.description: TOCTOU race on `exit_clean_used`: the check (`contains`) and the insert happen across two separate Mutex lock/unlock cycles (lines 642-646 and 671-674 in harden, 959-963 and 971-974 in review). If two concurrent ticks for the same loop_id execute between the two locks, both could pass the `contains` check and both would use exit_clean, violating the FR-7a one-shot guarantee. While the driver typically processes one tick per loop at a time, this is not guaranteed by the type system and the fix is trivial.
issues.suggestion: Acquire the lock once, check and insert atomically: `let mut guard = self.exit_clean_used.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()); if guard.contains(&record.id) { /* already used */ } else { guard.insert(record.id); /* proceed with exit_clean */ }`

issues.description: The `input_json` stored in judge_decisions includes the full spec_content (potentially tens of KB) for every judge invocation. With up to 10 judge calls per loop, this means the judge_decisions table can grow rapidly. More critically, the `serde_json::to_value(context)` serialization includes the `rounds` array which contains full verdict JSON from every prior round — this means each successive judge decision row stores O(n²) cumulative data. For a 15-round loop hitting the judge 10 times, this is substantial.
issues.suggestion: Consider storing only a summary or hash of spec_content in input_json rather than the full text, or at minimum document this as a known growth concern. Alternatively, store spec_content and rounds separately or as references rather than inlining them in every row.

issues.description: The `test_invoke_timeout_returns_none` test uses SlowJudgeClient (60s sleep) with a real 30s timeout. Without `tokio::time::pause()`, this test blocks for 30 real seconds. The dev-dependency already includes `tokio test-util` feature.
issues.suggestion: Add `tokio::time::pause()` at the start of the test to make `tokio::time::timeout` resolve instantly in virtual time.

issues.description: The `should_invoke` trigger priority silently resolves ambiguity when multiple triggers apply simultaneously (e.g., `not_clean AND max_rounds AND recurring_findings` all true). RecurringFindings wins over MaxRounds, which wins over NotClean. This is fine functionally (the judge gets full context regardless), but the priority ordering is implicit and undocumented.
issues.suggestion: Add a brief comment explaining the priority order and why RecurringFindings takes precedence (it's the most specific signal).

issues.description: FR-6c envisions judge_decisions rendered inline with round summaries in `nemo inspect` output. The CLI just dumps the full InspectResponse as raw JSON. While the data is present, it's not formatted for human consumption. Acceptable for Stage 1 but worth noting.
issues.suggestion: Consider adding a formatted rendering of judge decisions alongside round summaries in a future pass, or at minimum add a comment that this is intentionally deferred.
